root_text.an11.3:1.2: Avippaṭisāre asati avippaṭisāravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ.
root_text.an11.3:1.3: Pāmojje asati pāmojjavipannassa hatūpanisā hoti pīti.
root_text.an11.3:1.4: Pītiyā asati pītivipannassa hatūpanisā hoti passaddhi.
root_text.an11.3:1.5: Passaddhiyā asati passaddhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti sukhaṁ.
root_text.an11.3:1.6: Sukhe asati sukhavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti sammāsamādhi.
root_text.an11.3:1.7: Sammāsamādhimhi asati sammāsamādhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an11.3:1.8: Yathābhūtañāṇadassane asati yathābhūtañāṇadassanavipannassa hatūpanisā hoti nibbidā.
root_text.an11.3:1.9: Nibbidāya asati nibbidāvipannassa hatūpaniso hoti virāgo.
root_text.an11.3:1.10: Virāge asati virāgavipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an11.3:2.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rukkho sākhāpalāsavipanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi na pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an11.3:2.2: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dussīlassa sīlavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti avippaṭisāro,
root_text.an11.3:2.3: avippaṭisāre asati avippaṭisāravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ …pe…
root_text.an11.3:3.1: Sīlavato, bhikkhave, sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro,
root_text.an11.3:3.2: avippaṭisāre sati avippaṭisārasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ,
root_text.an11.3:3.3: pāmojje sati pāmojjasampannassa upanisasampannā hoti pīti,
root_text.an11.3:3.4: pītiyā sati pītisampannassa upanisasampannā hoti passaddhi,
root_text.an11.3:3.5: passaddhiyā sati passaddhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti sukhaṁ,
root_text.an11.3:3.6: sukhe sati sukhasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti sammāsamādhi,
root_text.an11.3:3.7: sammāsamādhimhi sati sammāsamādhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ,
root_text.an11.3:3.8: yathābhūtañāṇadassane sati yathābhūtañāṇadassanasampannassa upanisasampannā hoti nibbidā,
root_text.an11.3:3.9: nibbidāya sati nibbidāsampannassa upanisasampanno hoti virāgo,
root_text.an11.3:3.10: virāge sati virāgasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an11.3:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rukkho sākhāpalāsasampanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an11.3:4.2: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sīlavato sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro,

translation_text.an11.3:1.1: “Mendicants, an unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for having no regrets.
translation_text.an11.3:1.2: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an11.3:1.3: When there is no joy, one who lacks joy has destroyed a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an11.3:1.4: When there is no rapture, one who lacks rapture has destroyed a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an11.3:1.5: When there is no tranquility, one who lacks tranquility has destroyed a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an11.3:1.6: When there is no bliss, one who lacks bliss has destroyed a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an11.3:1.7: When there is no right immersion, one who lacks right immersion has destroyed a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an11.3:1.8: When there is no true knowledge and vision, one who lacks true knowledge and vision has destroyed a vital condition for disillusionment.
translation_text.an11.3:1.9: When there is no disillusionment, one who lacks disillusionment has destroyed a vital condition for dispassion.
translation_text.an11.3:1.10: When there is no dispassion, one who lacks dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.3:2.1: Suppose there was a tree that lacked branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would not grow to fullness.
translation_text.an11.3:2.2: In the same way, an unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for having no regrets.
translation_text.an11.3:2.3: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an11.3:2.4: When there is no dispassion, one who lacks dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.3:3.1: An ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an11.3:3.2: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an11.3:3.3: When there is joy, one who has fulfilled joy has fulfilled a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an11.3:3.4: When there is rapture, one who has fulfilled rapture has fulfilled a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an11.3:3.5: When there is tranquility, one who has fulfilled tranquility has fulfilled a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an11.3:3.6: When there is bliss, one who has fulfilled bliss has fulfilled a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an11.3:3.7: When there is right immersion, one who has fulfilled right immersion has fulfilled a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an11.3:3.8: When there is true knowledge and vision, one who has fulfilled true knowledge and vision has fulfilled a vital condition for disillusionment.
translation_text.an11.3:3.9: When there is disillusionment, one who has fulfilled disillusionment has fulfilled a vital condition for dispassion.
translation_text.an11.3:3.10: When there is dispassion, one who has fulfilled dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.3:4.1: Suppose there was a tree that was complete with branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would grow to fullness.
translation_text.an11.3:4.2: In the same way, an ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an11.3:4.3: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an11.3:4.4: When there is dispassion, one who has fulfilled dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.”
